title_en: Introduction: Al-Wasitiyyah and Its Place in Islamic Scholarship
title_ar: المقدمة: الواسطية ومكانتها في الفكر الإسلامي
content_en:
Sheikh Muhammad ibn Salih al-Uthaymin opens his commentary on Ibn Taymiyyah's al-Aqeedah al-Wasitiyyah with a detailed introduction that contextualizes the original text and explains why this particular work has occupied such a central place in the teaching of Athari aqeedah for seven centuries.

Al-Aqeedah al-Wasitiyyah was written by Sheikh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah (d. 728 AH) in response to a request from a judge (qadi) from Wasit in Iraq who asked him to compose a treatise representing the creed of Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah. Ibn Taymiyyah wrote it in a single sitting — an indication of how completely its contents were a living part of his knowledge — and it became one of the most widely studied and taught texts in Islamic theological education. Al-Uthaymin's commentary, delivered as a series of lessons, is among the most accessible and comprehensive explanations of this important text.

Al-Uthaymin explains that al-Wasitiyyah holds its central position for several reasons. First, it is a concise but comprehensive summary of Ahl us-Sunnah's positions on the most contested theological questions — divine attributes, the Quran, the Day of Judgment, the Companions, and the Islamic understanding of political authority. Second, it derives all of its positions directly from the Quran and the authenticated Sunnah, without importing philosophical categories. Third, Ibn Taymiyyah presents the positions of Ahl us-Sunnah in contrast to the deviant groups — the Ash'ariyyah on the question of attributes, the Mu'tazilah on divine will and the Quran, the Khawarij and Murji'ah on iman — making it a comprehensive orientation map of the theological landscape.

Al-Uthaymin describes his own methodology in the commentary: he will explain the text sentence by sentence, clarify the vocabulary, expand on the arguments, provide additional Quranic and hadith evidence where relevant, and address contemporary misunderstandings or challenges to the positions Ibn Taymiyyah states. He notes that his goal is not scholarly elaboration for its own sake but the practical understanding of the student — the text should be internalized as a living creed, not merely studied as an academic exercise.

The Sheikh also explains Ibn Taymiyyah's definition of 'Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah' as it appears in the opening of al-Wasitiyyah. 'Ahl us-Sunnah' refers to those who hold firmly to the Sunnah of the Prophet and refuse to depart from it in matters of aqeedah. 'Al-Jama'ah' refers to the community of the Prophet's Companions and those who followed their way. Together, the phrase describes those who follow the collective, documented, transmitted understanding of the Prophet's Companions — as opposed to those who introduced innovations based on philosophical reasoning or deviant spiritual practices.

Al-Uthaymin emphasizes that the study of aqeedah precedes and conditions the study of everything else. A Muslim whose aqeedah is sound will approach the Quran correctly, will evaluate juristic opinions correctly, will relate to scholars and to Islamic history correctly. A Muslim whose aqeedah is distorted — even if unintentionally — will have that distortion affect his entire religious life. This is why Ibn Taymiyyah's text begins not with rulings on prayer or zakah but with the most foundational question of all: what is the correct belief concerning Allah, His attributes, and His religion?
title_id: Pengantar: Al-Wasitiyyah dan Kedudukannya dalam Beasiswa Islam
content_id:
Syaikh Muhammad ibn Shalih al-Utsaimin membuka komentarnya tentang Al-Aqidah al-Wasithiyyah karya Ibn Taymiyyah dengan pengantar terperinci yang menempatkan teks asli dan menjelaskan mengapa karya khusus ini menduduki tempat yang begitu sentral dalam pengajaran akidah Athari selama tujuh abad.

Al-Aqidah al-Wasithiyyah ditulis oleh Syaikh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah (wafat 728 H) sebagai respons terhadap permintaan seorang hakim (qadhi) dari Wasith di Irak yang memintanya untuk menyusun sebuah risalah yang mewakili akidah Ahlus Sunnah wal Jama'ah. Ibn Taymiyyah menulisnya dalam satu kali duduk — suatu pertanda betapa isinya adalah bagian yang hidup dari pengetahuannya — dan karya ini menjadi salah satu teks yang paling banyak dipelajari dan diajarkan dalam pendidikan teologi Islam. Komentar al-Utsaimin, yang disampaikan sebagai serangkaian pelajaran, termasuk yang paling mudah diakses dan paling komprehensif atas teks penting ini.

Al-Utsaimin menjelaskan bahwa Al-Wasithiyyah menempati posisi sentral karena beberapa alasan. Pertama, karya ini adalah ringkasan yang ringkas namun komprehensif tentang posisi-posisi Ahlus Sunnah atas pertanyaan-pertanyaan teologis yang paling diperdebatkan — sifat-sifat ilahi, Al-Qur'an, Hari Kiamat, para sahabat, dan pemahaman Islam tentang otoritas politik. Kedua, karya ini menurunkan semua posisinya langsung dari Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah yang telah diautentikasi, tanpa mengimpor kategori-kategori filosofis. Ketiga, Ibn Taymiyyah menyajikan posisi-posisi Ahlus Sunnah dalam kontras dengan kelompok-kelompok yang menyimpang — Asy'ariyyah dalam masalah sifat-sifat, Mu'tazilah tentang kehendak ilahi dan Al-Qur'an, Khawarij dan Murji'ah tentang iman — menjadikannya peta orientasi komprehensif tentang lanskap teologis.

Al-Utsaimin menggambarkan metodologinya sendiri dalam komentar: beliau akan menjelaskan teks kalimat demi kalimat, mengklarifikasi kosakata, memperluas argumen-argumen, menyediakan bukti Al-Qur'an dan hadis tambahan di mana relevan, dan menangani kesalahpahaman atau tantangan kontemporer terhadap posisi-posisi yang disajikan Ibn Taymiyyah. Komentar ini mewakili transmisi hidup dari tradisi Athari — tidak sekadar teks yang diulang melainkan ajaran yang dipahami, dihayati, dan disampaikan oleh seorang ulama yang telah mendedikasikan hidupnya untuk memahami dan mengajarkan akidah Ahlus Sunnah.
